name: Smooth Four-Dimensional Poincaré Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The closed 4-dimensional Poincaré sphere — the boundary that contains all smooth structures and their equivalence classes under diffeomorphism.

VINIT.justification: Before any smooth structure is imposed, the 4-dimensional space exists as pure potential — void, unnamed, awaiting differentiation.

TANCH.justification: The terminal anchor: the closed boundary that contains all smooth 4-manifolds satisfying the Poincaré conjecture. It is the hermetic vessel of the theorem.

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: the directed transformation from topological manifold to smooth manifold via atlas and transition functions. Growth of differentiability.

AREV.justification: Reverse morphism: descent from smooth manifold back to underlying topological manifold. Coagula — stripping away the differential layer.

CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining: if φ: M → N and ψ: N → P are diffeomorphisms, then ψ ∘ φ: M → P is their composition. Root→derived form.

IMSCRIB.justification: Self-reference: the element that maps M to itself. The stone that knows itself. Every manifold is diffeomorphic to itself.

FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication: one smooth 4-manifold M branches into two distinct paths: (A) simply-connected with trivial homology, (B) non-simply-connected with non-trivial homology.

FFUSE.justification: Multiplication: the two branches (simply-connected and non-simply-connected) are fused back via the Poincaré duality isomorphism H_k(M) ≅ H_{4-k}(M), recovering the original manifold M.

EVALT.justification: Affirmative branch: the positive evaluation — the case where the smooth 4-manifold is diffeomorphic to the 4-sphere. Success state.

EVALF.justification: Negative branch: the failure case — a smooth 4-manifold that is homeomorphic but NOT diffeomorphic to S⁴. Exotic differential structure.

ENGAGR.justification: Paradice: both simultaneously held without resolution. A smooth 4-manifold that is simultaneously diffeomorphic and non-diffeomorphic to S⁴ under different gauge-theoretic framings. The albedo stage of smooth topology.

IFIX.justification: ROM fixation: the Kirby diagram is an append-only, irreversible record of the smooth 4-manifold's handle decomposition. Once drawn, it cannot be undone — it is the permanent witness.

phase_2.split_element: FSPLIT: branching into homology equivalence classes
split_outputs: non-simply-connected branch: H_*(M) with non-trivial π₁(M)
phase_2.fuse_result: original smooth 4-manifold M recovered via H_k(M) ≅ H_{4-k}(M)

phase_3.void_description: 00 — the uninitialized 4-dimensional continuum before any smooth atlas is chosen. No differential structure, no tangent spaces, no curvature.
phase_3.true_description: 01 — the simply-connected smooth 4-manifold diffeomorphic to S⁴. Poincaré conjecture satisfied. Success state.
phase_3.false_description: 10 — exotic smooth structure on ℝ⁴ or a non-simply-connected 4-manifold. Diffeomorphically distinct from S⁴. Failure of naive expectation.
phase_3.both_description: 11 — Donaldson paradice: a smooth 4-manifold simultaneously satisfying and violating diffeomorphic equivalence to S⁴ under different gauge-theoretic or exotic framings. Both states held without resolution.

steps.domain_action: unstructured 4-dimensional continuum ∅ — void before topology is named
Poincaré homology sphere S⁴ — the closed boundary containing all smooth 4-manifolds
smooth atlas imposition — forward morphism from topological to smooth structure
composition of transition functions — chaining local diffeomorphisms into global structure
identity diffeomorphism id_M — self-recognition of the manifold
branch into homology equivalence classes — one manifold splits into simply-connected (T-arm) and non-simply-connected (F-arm) paths
simply-connected evaluation — T-arm anchored by affirmative: H_*(M) ≅ H_*(S⁴)
forward morphism on T-arm — strengthening simply-connected structure toward S⁴
composition of diffeomorphisms on T-arm — chaining toward canonical form
identity on T-arm — the simply-connected manifold recognizes itself as S⁴
exotic structure evaluation — F-arm anchored by negative: non-diffeomorphic to S⁴
reverse morphism on F-arm — descent into exotic differential structure
composition of exotic diffeomorphisms on F-arm — chaining through non-standard smoothings
identity on F-arm — the exotic manifold recognizes itself as homeomorphic but not diffeomorphic
Poincaré duality isomorphism — T-arm and F-arm reconstitute via H_k(M) ≅ H_{4-k}(M)
Donaldson invariant paradice — both T-arm and F-arm simultaneously held in gauge-theoretic superposition
Kirby diagram fixation — permanent append-only record of handle decomposition
composition of Kirby moves — sequential handle slides recorded irreversibly
self-reference loop — the Kirby diagram recognizes itself as the canonical witness
second fixation — Donaldson polynomial invariant recorded permanently
reverse morphism — descent from smooth structure back to topological substrate
composition of forgetting operations — stripping differential layers
identity on topological base — the underlying topological manifold recognizes itself
return to Poincaré sphere boundary — all smooth structures contained and closed

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Smooth atlas compiler — translates topological manifold into smooth structure via transition functions and Jacobian matrices. Compiles Poincaré duality into homology isomorphisms.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Diffeomorphism messaging — components (simply-connected and exotic branches) communicate via Poincaré duality isomorphism H_k ≅ H_{4-k}. Gauge-theoretic signals carry Donaldson invariants.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Kirby calculus store — smooth 4-manifold structure stored as handle decomposition diagram. Homology groups stored as finitely-generated abelian groups. Diffeomorphism classes indexed by exotic structure invariants.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Morse theory scheduler — determines order of critical points and handle attachments. Schedules branching into simply-connected vs. exotic paths. Timing governed by Floer homology flow.
phase_5.alfs_store: Smooth Poincaré sphere S⁴ — the canonical reference. All smooth 4-manifolds are measured against S⁴. Bootstrap store: the theorem itself (simply-connected smooth 4-manifold ⟹ diffeomorphic to S⁴).

phase_6.cycle_cost: Gauge-theoretic computation cost — each Donaldson invariant calculation consumes moduli space integration. Each Kirby move consumes one handle slide operation.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Unstructured 4-dimensional continuum with arbitrary smooth atlas — high entropy, many exotic structures possible.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Smooth 4-manifold classified by Poincaré duality and Donaldson invariants — entropy reduced to finite set of equivalence classes.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The cycle is entropy-conservative because Poincaré duality is an isomorphism (bijection): information is neither created nor destroyed, only reorganized. The branching (FSPLIT) into simply-connected and exotic paths is balanced by the fusion (FFUSE) via homology isomorphism. Kirby calculus moves are reversible up to isotopy, preserving topological information. The paradice (ENGAGR) holds both states simultaneously without dissipation — a quantum-like superposition of smooth structures.
